label: Nicole Boyd's State of Tribes Address

comments.text: Honored to hear from Chairwoman Nicole Boyd of the Red Cliff Band of Lake Superior Chippewa at the State of the Tribes address about tribal priorities and opportunities ahead. Senate Democrats are committed to respecting Tribal Nations as sovereign governments and continuing our strong partnership.

Chairwoman Nicole Boyd of the Red Cliff Band of Lake Superior Chippewa called for stronger tribal-state partnerships, water protections, economic growth, and recognition of wild rice as Wisconsin’s official grain during the 2026 State of the Tribes Address.
HAPPENING NOW: Red Cliff Band of Lake Superior Chippewa Indians Chair Nicole Boyd delivers the 2026 State of the Tribes Address from the Wisconsin State Capitol in Madison.
Red Cliff Band of Lake Superior Chippewa Indians Chair Nicole Boyd gave a prescient, standing room only State of the Tribes address yesterday.

Today, Chairwoman Nicole Boyd from the Red Cliff Band of Lake Superior Chippewa delivered a powerful and moving State of the Tribes address on the floor of the Assembly. This was the 26th such address. These moments should be ones that bring us together, and I’m grateful for them. Boyd’s speech…

Today, Chairwoman Nicole Boyd from the Red Cliff Band of Lake Superior Chippewa delivered the 2026 State of the Tribes. It was a privilege to hear about the contributions, challenges, and triumphs of Wisconsin’s sovereign tribal nations in this year’s address to members of the legislature.

Watch: 📺 🖥️ 💻 📱 Red Cliff Band of Lake Superior Chippewa Chairwoman Nicole Boyd discusses the relationship between tribal governments in Wisconsin and Gov. Tony Evers, who is completing his final year in office.

Watch: 📺 🖥️ 💻 📱 Red Cliff Band of Lake Superior Chippewa Chairwoman Nicole Boyd discusses actions by tribal nations responding to moves by the federal government to cut funds for health, nutrition and other services.

It was an honor to hear from Chairwoman Nicole Boyd of the Red Cliff Band of Lake Superior Chippewa, who delivered a powerful State of the Tribes address highlighting tribal priorities and challenges like clean water, quality education and opportunities for future generations.

#ICYMI: In yesterday's State of the Tribes, Red Cliff Band of Lake Superior Chippewa Indians Chair Nicole Boyd called for action on key issues like healthcare and clean water. WI Republicans are sitting on a budget surplus. It’s time they act. More ⬇️
Nicole Boyd, tribal chairwoman of the Red Cliff Band of Lake Superior Chippewa Indians, delivered the 2026 Wisconsin State of the Tribes address at the Capitol.

Tomorrow’s annual State of the Tribes address is a great opportunity to hear from Wisconsin’s sovereign tribal nations, and I’m excited to hear from Chairwoman Nicole Boyd of the Red Cliff Band of Lake Superior Chippewa. Tune in at 11:00 AM on February 10th to hear the address at wiseye.org/live

GUESTS Brenda Nicolas (Zapotec) University of California, Irvine Arcenio Lopez (Ñuu Savi) Mixteco/Indígena Community Organizing Project (MICOP) Desiree Tody (Red Cliff Band of Lake Superior Chippewa), Center Against Sexual & Domestic Abuse Joaquín Baca, Albuquerque City Council
